title: Seraphim forms space advisory council
summary: Seraphim Space, an early-stage space investor, has established a global advisory council comprising industry, policy, and investment leaders to guide its long-term strategy amid rapid geopolitical and technological changes in the sector.

title: Meink: Space Force must ‘execute’ as budget set to surge
summary: The U.S. Space Force must focus on execution despite workforce shortages and acquisition delays, as its budget is set to significantly increase. Service leaders emphasize the need to overcome bottlenecks to deliver new technologies effectively.

title: Defining acquisition on a wartime footing
summary: The U.S. space industry is shifting to a wartime footing due to changing geopolitical and strategic conditions, with top officials emphasizing the urgency of rapid acquisition. This move reflects a direct response to evolving threats and the need for accelerated development in the space sector.

title: NASA seeks proposals for commercial TDRSS replacement
summary: NASA is seeking proposals for commercial communications services to replace its aging TDRSS relay satellites, aiming for backward compatibility with existing systems. This initiative is part of the broader Commercial Services Project to transition from government-owned to commercially operated space infrastructure.

title: Emerson Launches Innovative Software-Driven Method for High-Fidelity Aerospace RF Testing
summary: Emerson introduced the NI Channel Emulator System Software (CHESS) platform at the 2026 Space Symposium, offering a software-defined method for high-fidelity aerospace RF testing. This innovation aims to enhance the validation of mission-critical radio frequency systems for aerospace and defense applications.

title: Saltzman unveils blueprint for Space Force’s future
summary: General Saltzman introduced the Space Force's 'Future Operating Environment' and 'Objective Force' documents, outlining a contested space domain and emphasizing the need for a combat-ready force. The blueprint aims to prepare the Space Force for future challenges in space operations.

title: Senators seek increased funding for NASA Mars missions
summary: Senators are pushing for increased funding for NASA's Mars missions to prevent severe harm to robotic exploration efforts. The request comes as part of budget negotiations to ensure continued scientific progress.

title: The Infrastructure Layer U.S. Space Superiority Has Been Missing
summary: The U.S. is missing a critical infrastructure layer for space superiority due to inadequate space domain awareness data, which lacks the right information, timely delivery, and autonomy for effective action.

title: Rethinking cyber and space with Joe Mazur
summary: The podcast 'Space Minds' features a discussion between Mike Gruss and Joe Mazur of Nightwing about the growing intersection of cybersecurity and space operations. The conversation highlights the acceleration of cyber operations in the space domain.

title: Novaspace and OGC Unveil the China Space Industry Intelligence Catalog
summary: Novaspace and Orbital Gateway Consulting (OGC) have launched the China Space Industry Intelligence Catalog to provide global stakeholders with data-driven insights into China's growing space market. The catalog aims to offer clear and comprehensive intelligence on the sector.

title: SpaceX fires up next-gen 'Version 3' Starship ahead of landmark May test flight (photos)
summary: SpaceX successfully completed the first full-duration static fire test of the upper stage of its next-gen 'Version 3' Starship, paving the way for a planned test flight next month.

title: How do supergiant exoplanets form? James Webb Space Telescope finds a clue
summary: Astronomers used the James Webb Space Telescope to study the giant exoplanet 29 Cygni b, which may help clarify the distinction between planets and stars. The findings could provide insights into the formation of supergiant exoplanets.

title: Space combat was 'critical to mission success' in US war in Iran, Space Force chief says
summary: The U.S. Space Force chief stated that space combat was crucial to mission success during the ongoing war in Iran, affirming the Space Force's combat readiness. This highlights the growing role of space assets in modern military operations.

title: NASA astronauts joke around with an optical illusion on the ISS | Space photo of the day for April 15, 2026
summary: Two NASA astronauts playfully pretended to hold a robotic cargo spacecraft during its capture on the ISS on April 13, 2026. The moment was shared as part of NASA's Space Photo of the Day.

title: Fly higher and longer for less with $120 off this sub-9-ounce Potensic Atom 2 drone
summary: Potensic Atom 2 drone is being offered at a 25% discount, providing up to 90 minutes of flight time with three batteries included. The lightweight drone is marketed for recreational use during spring and summer.

title: A dark energy tool just created the most comprehensive 3D map of our universe ever: 'This is a major paradigm shift'
summary: The Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument has created the most comprehensive 3D map of the universe, marking a significant scientific achievement. The mission, which lasted five years, continues to explore the cosmos.

title: Interstellar invader 3I/ATLAS is spraying tons of water into space every second. Jupiter-bound spacecraft JUICE discovers
summary: The interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS is ejecting tons of water into space daily, equivalent to filling 70 swimming pools. The Jupiter-bound spacecraft JUICE made this discovery.

title: Go behind the scenes of NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission with NOVA's 'Return to the Moon' documentary tonight (interview)
summary: PBS will air the NOVA documentary 'Return to the Moon' on April 15, providing a behind-the-scenes look at NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission.

title: Solar wind travels up to 4 times faster than expected, eclipse spacecraft reveals
summary: The Solar Orbiter spacecraft observed solar wind gusts in the inner corona moving three to four times faster than expected. This discovery challenges previous assumptions about solar wind speeds.

title: I Am Artemis: Rebekah Tolatovicz
summary: Rebekah Tolatovicz, a mechanical technician lead, has contributed to building, integrating, and testing multiple Orion spacecraft for NASA's Artemis program at Kennedy Space Center. She works under Lockheed Martin, the main contractor for the Orion spacecraft.

title: NASA Selects Voyager for Seventh Private Mission to Space Station
summary: NASA and Voyager Technologies have signed an order for the seventh private astronaut mission to the International Space Station, targeting a launch no earlier than 2028 from Florida. This marks Voyager Technologies' first selection for such a mission, highlighting NASA's push to grow the commercial space economy.

title: NASA Launches Six CubeSats to International Space Station
summary: NASA launched six CubeSats to the International Space Station as part of a resupply mission using Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL spacecraft. The CubeSats were built by U.S. educational institutions and included in the 11,000 pounds of cargo.

title: 2026 NSTA Hyperwall Schedule
summary: NASA has announced its Hyperwall schedule for the 2026 NSTA event, featuring presentations by experts like Christina Milotte and Tina Harte Ballinger from April 16-18, 2026. Topics include space weather education for Artemis missions and NASA resources like 5E StoryMaps.

title: NASA’s SPHEREx Mission Maps Water Ice Throughout Cygnus X
summary: NASA's SPHEREx mission has mapped water ice and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons in Cygnus X, a highly active star-forming region in the Milky Way. The observations highlight the chemical composition of this turbulent area.

title: ‘Interstellar Glaciers’: NASA’s SPHEREx Maps Vast Galactic Ice Regions
summary: NASA's SPHEREx mission has mapped vast interstellar ice regions within the Milky Way galaxy, covering areas over 600 light-years across. The ice was discovered inside giant molecular clouds, which are dense regions of gas and dust.

title: Artemis II Crew Returns to Houston
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew returned to Houston after landing at Ellington Airport. The crew, including astronauts from NASA and CSA, shared remarks with the crowd.

title: Honoring Alex Goetz, a Landsat Legend
summary: Dr. Alex Goetz, a key figure in Landsat science and a member of the Landsat 7 Science Team, passed away in 2025. He is remembered as a Landsat legend.

title: Metrics
summary: NASA has released the FY26 Services Catalog and Service Level Agreement (SLA) for NSSC services, detailing service descriptions, rates, and performance metrics.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew experienced vehicle departures and a spacewalk in March. The month was marked by high activity aboard the International Space Station.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Ship 39 Completes Static fire ahead of Booster 19 test Wednesday
summary: Ship 39 completed a static fire test ahead of the Booster 19 test scheduled for Wednesday. This marks a key milestone for the Artemis program following the successful return of Orion.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission completion.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II successfully completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than any previous crewed mission. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has introduced new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing capabilities and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing efforts to support future missions.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II set a new record by conducting a successful lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. These launches occur as NASA's Artemis II mission progresses.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific space industry news details are provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA is inviting SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session and an in-person event at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement has been highlighted as optimal for navigation purposes.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has created 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona observations. The mission's first results reveal that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology. It highlights improvements in precision and efficiency for satellite and probe navigation.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's parabolic flight campaign. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, showed that lasers can propel these ultralight materials, offering potential for future satellite and solar sail applications.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this innovative material for future space missions.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed with versatile capabilities, potentially revolutionizing multiple applications in the space industry. The chip's multifunctional design could enhance satellite systems and other aerospace technologies.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: ORBX ETF launches to track companies earning over half their revenue from space tech, including transportation, software, and satellite services. This follows a wave of space tech IPOs.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test the 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to demonstrate advanced space-based data transmission capabilities.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget challenges, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He emphasizes the importance of balancing scientific passion with practical financial planning.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) companies have disputed NASA's claims, to which NASA responded by challenging them to prove their objections wrong. The exchange took place during the Space Symposium.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered portal platform to help operators detect anomalies and make decisions efficiently. The platform serves as a centralized solution for space operations.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime has been launched as a new business unit led by a former Texas representative, focusing on collaborations with the Pentagon and NASA. The robotic construction company ICON announced this new initiative.

title: Exclusive: Vast Debuts Flight Suit For Haven-1, Private Astronaut Missions
summary: Vast has unveiled a flight suit designed for its upcoming Haven-1 space station and private astronaut missions. The company is reviving the trend of cargo pants as part of the suit's design.

title: Atomic-6 Launches Orbital Data Center Marketplace
summary: Atomic-6, a composite manufacturer based in Georgia, has launched ODC.Space, a marketplace to simplify securing orbital compute resources. This initiative aims to accelerate the growth of the orbital data center market.

title: Sophia Space and Kepler Sign Agreement to Demo ODC Tech
summary: Sophia Space and Kepler Communications have signed an agreement to demonstrate orbital data center (ODC) technology in space. This marks Sophia Space's first opportunity to validate its ODC software in an operational environment.

title: Hawkeye 360 Files For IPO
summary: Hawkeye 360, a space-based RF data and analytics firm, has filed for an IPO, aiming to raise capital as the defense-focused space sector expands. This move follows a trend of similar companies going public to fuel growth.

title: COMSO Targets Expanding Working Capital Fund and Determining CASR Funding
summary: Space Systems Command’s Commercial Space Office (COMSO) is aiming to expand its Working Capital Fund and determine funding for the Commercial Assessment of Space Resources (CASR). The initiative focuses on leveraging commercial space capabilities.

title: Teledyne Space Imaging President Megan Tremer Shares Enabling Tech for Artemis II
summary: Teledyne Space Imaging President Megan Tremer discussed the technologies provided by Teledyne for the Artemis II mission during the Space Symposium. The conversation was featured in a Via Satellite article.

title: Moog Space SVP Mark Covelli Details Meteor Satellite Bus at Space Symposium
summary: Moog Space's senior vice president Mark Covelli discussed the company's support for the Artemis II mission and its investment in the Meteor satellite bus during the Space Symposium.

title: Lonestar Expands Launch Plans for Orbital Data Storage Payload
summary: Lonestar announced the launch date for StarVault, the world's first commercially operational space-based sovereign data storage platform. The company highlighted growing demand for off-planet data storage solutions.

title: US Space Command Chief Outlines Need for Maneuver Warfare Strategy
summary: US Space Command Chief emphasizes the need for a space maneuver warfare strategy due to intensified Chinese activities in orbit, including refueling and logistics operations.

title: Integrated Satcom-Terrestrial Gateway Solution for Resilient ATC Management and Modernization
summary: A new integrated satcom-terrestrial gateway solution is being developed to enhance resilient Air Traffic Control (ATC) management and modernization. This initiative aims to improve communication and operational efficiency in air traffic systems.

title: Comtech Achieves Major Milestone with Delivery of First EDIM Modems to U.S. Army
summary: Comtech has delivered the first EDIM modems to the U.S. Army, marking a significant milestone in military communications technology. This delivery advances the Army's tactical communications capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Closes Mynaric Acquisition at Higher Price Than Originally Agreed
summary: Rocket Lab completed its acquisition of Mynaric AG for $155.3 million, exceeding the original deal terms by $5.3 million. The purchase focuses on Mynaric's optical communications terminal technology.

title: Allied Space Strategies Stress Complementary Capabilities for Burden Sharing in Orbit
summary: Allied Space Strategies emphasizes the importance of burden sharing among allies and partners to enhance collective space capabilities, resilience, and deterrence. The focus is on leveraging complementary strengths to strengthen the space domain.

title: NASA Administrator Isaacman Hails the Success of Artemis, Previews Plans for Artemis III and IV
summary: NASA Administrator Isaacman praised the success of Artemis II and outlined plans for Artemis III and IV missions. The agency is accelerating efforts to build a lunar base.
